experiments.hypothesis: ETH 1h is the best-fit primary lane for testing whether a dominant session can create a short-lived continuation edge after price holds near session-led value. The edge is participation in broad follow-through, not blind breakout chasing.
experiments.expected_effect: pending: no backtest outcome recorded yet
experiments.lesson_extracted: Expected behavior: ETH 1h plus at least one confirmation lane should have produced a modest set of dominant-session second-leg continuation trades, showing whether session-led directional control survives in a mixed transition/chop intraday backdrop. Actual behavior: every queued screen run was discarded as orphan_or_legacy_cycle with null result_id before any evaluation occurred. The mismatch is execution-layer rather than market-layer, so there is no basis for refine, abort, or promote.

experiments.hypothesis: BTC 1h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing inventory-reset continuation after a flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic. The edge should come from repaired positioning and resumed drift back with the prior direction.
experiments.expected_effect: pending: no backtest outcome recorded yet
experiments.lesson_extracted: Expected behavior: BTC 1h flush-and-reclaim continuation should either prove real repaired-continuation density across BTC and a cross-lane or fail on genuine screen results. Actual behavior: all queued runs were orphan-discarded before any result existed, so there is no valid-data comparison between expected repaired continuation and realized market outcome. This is a system execution fault rather than a trading diagnosis such as too sparse or wrong regime.

experiments.hypothesis: AXS 1h is the structurally different lane for testing whether entering after expansion confirmation and then aggressively taking partials while keeping a small core can outperform all-in/all-out management. The edge is partly in management structure, not just the trigger.
experiments.expected_effect: pending: no backtest outcome recorded yet
experiments.lesson_extracted: Expected behavior: AXS 1h plus one confirmation lane should have shown whether post-expansion partial-profit runner management realizes frequent first targets with occasional residual runner contribution. Actual behavior: every queued screen run was discarded as orphan_or_legacy_cycle and no result_id was produced, so the management thesis never reached valid backtest evaluation. Because the failure happened before testing, the correct classification is fix_only.
